articles.title: Weather Today [15 April, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Snow, Heat Alert &. Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian

articles.full_text:
Weather Today, 15 April 2026: India is witnessing a rapid shift toward peak summer conditions as temperatures climb sharply across northern, central and southern regions. According to the India Meteorological Department, nearly 95% of the country is experiencing clear skies, accelerating daytime heating. Several cities are already approaching 40°C, while central and peninsular regions may see temperatures between 42°C and 45°C this week with warmer nights, falling humidity and fading western disturbances are further intensifying heat stress, particularly across urban centers.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
Light snowfall remains possible in higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh, especially in Srinagar and Gulmarg however, rainfall across plains remains limited with dry and hot conditions dominating most of India.
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
- Rising influence of climate variability and El Niño risks
These factors combined are pushing temperatures up faster than usual.
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
Delhi-NCR Weather Today
Minimum: 28°C | Maximum: 39°C
Delhi NCR is expected to experience dry heat with temperatures touching 40°C this week and winds remain light with clear skies.

Hot and humid conditions expected with coastal winds offering slight evening relief.

Moderate coastal heat continues with humidity above 65%.

FAQ’s: Weather Today Update
Are There Any Travel or Safety Warnings?
Yes, avoid afternoon travel and stay hydrated as heatwave conditions intensify.
Will Temperatures Rise Across the Country?
Yes, most regions may see temperatures between 40°C–45°C this week.
Will There Be Any Rain Today?
Rain chances remain low across most plains.
Where Will It Snow Today?
Higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir may see light snowfall.
Is Heatwave Likely to Continue?
Yes, weather experts expect above-normal heat in coming weeks.
